opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My right side feels like it's being continuously electrocuted and burned with a hot iron. I thought I was having a heart attack for the last few days, until these blisters showed up on that same side today.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The burning pain has been unbearable for three days and now a blistering rash has come up on the same side of my chest, and I'm frightened it's my heart.

symptom_narrative.onset: the burning pain started about three days ago; the rash only appeared today on the same side
symptom_narrative.duration: three days of pain, rash for less than a day and still coming out
symptom_narrative.character: a constant deep burning and electric-shock sensation in the skin of the right chest; the skin feels raw and hypersensitive even to a shirt touching it
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst pain I've had — I'm taking shallow breaths because breathing deeply makes the chest hurt more
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: a band across the right side of my chest, from my back around towards the middle of my front, all on the one side
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant, does not come and go
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing has helped; the painkillers I have at home did nothing
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any touch on the skin, and taking a deep breath
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: feeling generally run down, tired and a bit feverish; small fluid-filled blisters on a red base that are still appearing
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: had chickenpox as a child; nothing like this before

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: over-the-counter paracetamol taken at home with no relief

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with partner who is present and helping make the call; recently been under a lot of stress and tired

objective_data_offered.value: a band of small grouped clear fluid-filled blisters sitting on a bright red base, running across the right side of the chest and stopping at the middle of the front; all strictly on one side
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: appearance over a video/photo only; the clinician cannot map the exact nerve territory or feel the skin remotely

objective_data_offered.value: 37.6 C on a home thermometer this afternoon
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on an uncalibrated home thermometer

objective_data_offered.value: partner says he is guarding the right side of his chest, taking shallow breaths, and is very distressed by the pain
blisters are uncovered; some are clear and a few look like they are starting to cloud over
